extraction.one_line_summary: VAN DER POEL Mathieu won solo after dropping van Aert when Jumbo-Visma's leader flatted in the closing kilometers, recording near-record 46kph average speed.
tactical_patterns: VAN DER POEL capitalized immediately when van Aert flatted in front group late, no teammates waited—pure opportunism wins Roubaix.
Alpecin put van der Poel in winning position despite van Aert being stronger rival, team execution decisive over individual power.
PHILIPSEN sprinted from chase group for second using track positioning skills, inside line technique beat van Aert's raw power.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: mechanical flat tire in front group with van der Poel late in race cost him realistic victory chance
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: never finished top-10 in one-day classic before, took second via superior velodrome track craft against van Aert
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: neutral: riding 13th consecutive Roubaix finish, suggesting durability rather than competitiveness at this career stage
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: crashed inexplicably early in race, continued pattern from Flanders crash week prior, signaling declining bike handling
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: neutral: made early breakaway for Israel Premier Tech but commentary suggests no impact on final
